title: Roupala monosperma
taxonomy: Roupala monosperma belongs to the family of plants known as Lauraceae, a family that includes approximately 70-90 genera of evergreen trees and shrubs. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is classified under the scientific name Roupala monosperma. Roupala monosperma is classified as a species within the genus Roupala, which is part of the family Lauraceae. The Lauraceae family is further classified under the larger group called Magnoliidae, which includes approximately 9 families of flowering plants. The classification of Roupala monosperma reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Lauraceae family.

intro: Roupala monosperma, also known as the "Roupala tree" or "Brazilian rosewood", is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic effects.
